mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of > 0.5 percent coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 70% of observations during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which mechanically boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns with a rank correlation below -0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, persistent inflation forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0 are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates synchronized demand across production and consumption sectors, reinforcing earnings revision cycles and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day forward returns across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, triggering immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of instances, breaking the supply-contraction sell-off channel.
